message:
fix(lab/network): use full-width u128 random value for latency and jitter range sampling

LatencyModel::Uniform, JitterModel::Uniform, and JitterModel::Bursty all
computed random offsets via u128::from(rng.next_u64()) % (range + 1). When
the duration range exceeds u64::MAX nanoseconds (~584 years), the u64 source
could not cover the full range, introducing modulo bias at the top. More
practically, even for sub-u64 ranges the single-call path had a slight
low-bit bias for ranges that are not powers of two.

Now combines two u64 calls into a full u128:
  (u128::from(rng.next_u64()) << 64) | u128::from(rng.next_u64())

This eliminates the coverage gap for large ranges and reduces modulo bias
for all range sizes. Applied to all three sampling sites in config.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
